data.title: Secured UK Road Funding Pipeline Underpins Hill & Smith’s Medium-Term Earnings Recovery and Buy Rating
data.description:
David Farrell, an analyst from Jefferies, maintained the Buy rating on Hill & Smith Holdings. The associated price target remains the same with p2,760.00.

Easter Sale - 70% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

David Farrell’s rating is based on the view that the latest U.K. Road Investment Strategy, while flat in nominal terms versus the prior period, still secures a substantial multiyear funding pipeline for strategic roads. This long-term visibility underpins demand for Hill & Smith’s U.K. roads-exposed operations and supports management’s efforts to improve the performance of the seven U.K.-focused businesses within the UK & India Engineered Solutions division.

Farrell also notes that, despite recent mid-single-digit organic revenue declines and margins that remain well below the group’s target, the confirmed programme reduces uncertainty and should help stabilise and ultimately enhance profitability. With a clearer workload outlook and the potential for operational improvements as the company executes on this secured pipeline, he believes the current valuation does not fully reflect the medium-term earnings recovery, justifying a Buy recommendation.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk

data.title: CommVault: Strategic Takeover Interest and SaaS Transition Underscore Undervalued Buy Opportunity
data.description:
CommVault Systems, the Technology sector company, was revisited by a Wall Street analyst yesterday. Analyst Shrenik Kothari from Robert W. Baird assigned a Buy rating on the stock and has a $160.00 price target.

Easter Sale - 70% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Shrenik Kothari has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors tied to CommVault’s strategic position and current valuation. He highlights that takeover interest from both private equity and strategic buyers, including a noted software-focused sponsor, signals that the market increasingly recognizes CommVault as a core cyber resilience and data protection platform rather than a low-growth legacy backup vendor.

At the same time, he points to the company’s growing base of subscription and SaaS customers, rising SaaS ARR mix, and strong net dollar retention as evidence of durable, recurring revenue and an improving business mix. Coupled with shares trading at a discount to peers on sales and free cash flow multiples despite this transition, he sees a supportive risk/reward profile and a potential valuation floor, which together justify maintaining a positive, Buy-oriented stance on the stock.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk
